name: Olde Tymes Famous Sausage Gravy
recipeInstructions:
1. Mix water and milk in a stock pot.
2. Place the pot on the stove to heat, but do not boil.
3. Chop the sausage: if using links, process them through a food chopper; if using bulk, chop and stir while cooking.
4. Fry the chopped sausage in a cast iron skillet until browned.
5. Carefully strain the cooked sausage fat into the skillet over moderate heat.
6. Add the cooked sausage to the milk and water mixture.
7. In a separate pan, make a roux by stirring flour, salt, and pepper until well combined.
8. Cook the roux until it clings to the spoon and comes off the skillet cleanly.
9. Slowly stir in the roux into the milk and water mixture while turning off the heat to thicken the gravy.
10. Serve over biscuits or toast.
